answer:
### INCIDENT SUMMARY
Summary: The policy allows for explicit consent-based collection of 'Name' and 'Phone Number' during account registration. Collection context is during Background Telemetry.

### DETAILED ANALYSIS

• The policy explicitly mentions that information collected at the time of registration includes "Name" (Section [aarogya_setu#C5]) and "Phone Number" (Section [aarogya_setu#C5]).
• The collection is consent-based, as users are requested to provide their consent for utilizing their privacy policy (Section [aarogya_setu#C17]).
• There is no mention of any umbrella term that would authorize the collection of 'Name' and 'Phone Number' without explicit consent.
• The policy does not contradict itself by promising privacy while collecting invasive telemetry.

### AUDITOR CONCLUSION
Based on the provided policy context, it can be concluded that the collection of 'Name' and 'Phone Number' during account registration is authorized due to explicit consent.
